meaning: n.鏈夋剰澶栧彂鐜扮弽瀹濈殑杩愭皵
examples: The word "serendipity" was coined by Horace Walpole in an 1854 letter, from a tale of three princes who "were always making discoveries, by accident, of things they were not in search of".
Some of the best effects in my garden have been the result of serendipity.
This is the kind of serendipity you want!
Photographers and travellers have to stumble upon each other in a bit of serendipity.
But rather than intent-driven searches like those on Amazon, Fancy is all about serendipity.
